#+TITLE: Make the refresh impossible to miss
#+SUBTITLE: A temporary high-energy mode for launch-season communications that should feel branded, readable, and unmistakably refreshed.
#+AUTHOR: Hubert Behaghel
#+DATE: 2026-04-28
#+LANGUAGE: en
#+LATEX_CLASS: veriff
#+LATEX_VARIANT: refresh-overdrive
#+EXPORT_EYEBROW: Refresh mode · louder on purpose
#+EXPORT_FOOTER_NOTE: The overdrive question is simple: can the refresh be pushed this hard without undermining the sense of trust?

#+begin_standfirst
Trust is no longer a static promise. It is a system: observable, explainable, fast under pressure, and humane at the point of friction.
#+end_standfirst

#+ATTR_LATEX: :options [Operational proverb]
#+begin_epigraph
Proof is strongest when every decision can explain itself.
#+end_epigraph

* Trust without compromise

The strongest identity products no longer sell a check. They sell a posture. This means *bold* decisions, /italic/ emphasis, _underline_ for focus, and +strike-through+ for deprecated ideas. We evaluate the ~risk_score~ and set the =verbatim_flag= accordingly.

** Operating principle

The posture says: protect the business, preserve the customer moment, and make every decision legible enough to defend.

#+ATTR_LATEX: :options [Prototype testimonial · Global platform operator]
#+begin_quote
Trust has to feel engineered before it can feel effortless.
#+end_quote

#+begin_pullquote
What mattered was not only the fraud reduction. It was the sense that the platform could explain itself while still moving at business speed.
#+end_pullquote

* AI-powered architecture, built for trust

Under the surface, a trustworthy platform blends signal density, model intelligence, policy control, and operational feedback.

For external proof, teams can review the [[https://www.veriff.com/][Veriff trust platform]] and compare operational notes.[fn:trust-note]

- Confirm the user, document, and device story in one pass.
- Resolve the session using transparent policy guardrails.

The operating sequence stays simple:

1. Collect identity evidence.
2. Score signals against policy.
3. Explain the decision to operators.

The review checklist stays visible:

- [X] Link evidence to the decision record.
- [ ] Escalate ambiguous sessions for review.

#+ATTR_LATEX: :options [Design signal]
#+begin_callout
In the overdrive mode, the article becomes a deliberate piece of comms theatre. It still needs discipline, but it is allowed to be more obviously branded than the calmer variants.
#+end_callout

#+NAME: fig:brand-marker
#+CAPTION: A compact brand marker used as a native Org figure.

* Performance at scale

| Measure             | Signal        | Why it matters                   |
|---------------------+---------------+----------------------------------|
| Decision latency    | 6 seconds     | Keeps conversion momentum alive  |
| Coverage            | 230+ regions  | Expands commercial reach         |
| Verification fabric | 1,000+ signals | Increases confidence in decisions |

* Implementation note

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(defun hub/demo-route (session-id)
  "Approve the session if the risk score is acceptable."
  (let ((score (hub/risk-score session-id))
        (threshold 85))
    (if (>= score threshold)
        (message "Session %s approved with score %d" session-id score)
      (message "Session %s requires manual review" session-id))))
#+end_src

[fn:trust-note] Native Org footnotes should read as supporting notes, not as page chrome.

* Core concepts

- Risk engine :: The central system that evaluates signals and assigns a confidence score.
- Signal density :: The volume and quality of data points collected during a session.
- Policy control :: The ruleset that determines the threshold for automatic approval or manual review.
